title: astragalus barrelieri var. queraltii
intro: Astragalus barrelieri var. queraltii, also known as Queralt's milk-vetch, is a type of legume plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in the body's physiological processes. The plant's root and leaves contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, providing potential health benefits such as enhanced immune function, improved cardiovascular health, and reduced oxidative stress. Astragalus barrelieri var. queraltii can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
